notes: Real per-sentence `difficulty` labels are auto-derived from word count (easy<=7w / medium 8-12w / hard>=13w), NOT hand-tagged syntax. Control length distribution, not syntactic 'difficulty'.

dimensions.name: sentence_length_overall_words
examples: If you want to know what is being offered, just check the daily menu for special dishes
before you leave the classroom, be sure you got all your tools back into the toolbox
dimensions.gap: center matches; gen truncates the long tail (gen max 15 vs real 17-18)

dimensions.name: difficulty_tier_mix
dimensions.detector: per-7-set count of easy/medium/hard; signature histogram (hand-validated vs full arrays)
examples: 2026-01-21_repeat = 2/4/1 (e e m m m m h)
2026-04-29_repeat = 1/6/0 (all-medium-dominant)
dimensions.gap: BIGGEST GAP. Gen is rigid 2/3/2; over-produces hard (2 every set), under-produces medium. Real is medium-dominant and varies.

dimensions.name: length_progression
examples: 2026-01-21 lengths 5,7,8,10,12,9,14 — S6 dips, S7 longest
dimensions.gap: monotonic matches; gen slightly low on 'S7 longest'. Real signature = bumpy middle, long finish (not a staircase).

dimensions.name: s1_opener_type
dimensions.detector: regex-classify first sentence; hand-verified by reading all 51 S1
examples: Laptops are located in this aisle.
The library books are located here.
begin by mixing the butter and sugar
Welcome to our campus tour.
dimensions.gap: gen opens 'Welcome...' 64% (real 16%) and NEVER opens with a bare locating declarative (real 53%)

dimensions.name: sentence_mood_mix
examples: Check the bulletin board for information about guided tours.
The pond is a popular spot for sitting and relaxing.
dimensions.gap: balance close; only defect is the 5.2% questions (see yes_no_questions)

dimensions.name: yes_no_questions
dimensions.detector: ?-ending or aux-initial; validated 0 '?' anywhere in real
dimensions.current: 4/77 = 5.2%: 'Do you have your insurance card?', 'Are you here for math help?', 'Do you have a reservation?', 'Do you have your safety goggles?'
dimensions.gap: real has ZERO questions; gen invents yes/no Qs (seeded by prompt example 'Do you have your student ID?')

dimensions.name: contraction_rate
examples: When you're finished reading them, please place all your books in the return bin.
dimensions.gap: none in output; but validator's natural_spoken_register REWARDS contractions, mis-calibrated vs reality (low priority)

dimensions.name: direct_address_rate
examples: The pond is a popular spot for sitting and relaxing.
Bread is freshly baked on site every day.
dimensions.gap: gen over-addresses listener (53% vs 37%); many real sentences describe the place/objects, not 'you'

dimensions.name: hard_sentence_comma_rate
examples: If you need help getting around, check the map for specific areas and facilities
When you are ready, simply select Print from your computer and retrieve your document.
dimensions.gap: gen's long sentences under-punctuated; real long sentences usually carry a fronted clause break

dimensions.name: conditional_fronted_clause_rate
examples: If you are unsure when the lab is open, you can check the weekly schedule here
Lastly, please also remember to check the event schedule at the entrance
dimensions.gap: gen slightly high and concentrates on 'if'; real spreads across if/when/before/lastly

dimensions.name: punitive_warning_trope

distribution.real: 0/351 (4 keyword hits all false positives: 'free of charge', 'fine details')

dimensions.detector: /will result in|suspension|privileges|incur|penalt|violation/i; validated by broad punitive grep on raw text
dimensions.current: 8/77 = 10.4%: '...will result in a temporary suspension of your tutoring privileges.', 'If you return the car late, you will incur an extra daily charge.', 'Overdue books will result in fines...'
dimensions.gap: STRONGEST synthetic fingerprint. 0% real vs 10.4% gen. Seeded verbatim by prompt S7 example. Real 'rules' are gentle ('We hope everyone will ensure that books are returned on time.')

dimensions.name: closing_wayfinding_trope
dimensions.unit: share of sets whose last sentence references map/schedule/guide
dimensions.detector: keyword scan of final sentence per set
examples: If you need help getting around, check the map for specific areas and facilities
you can check the weekly schedule here
you can download the map for free
dimensions.current: 0/11 last sentences mention map/schedule/guide (gen closes on punitive rules instead)
dimensions.gap: positive real signature gen entirely lacks; ~1/3 of orientation sets should close on a 'check the map/schedule' pointer

dimensions.name: scenario_domain_mix
dimensions.target: include procedure/how-to family + everyday-retail/cooking/repair, not only campus offices
examples: first, select the right tool to remove the wheel (bike-repair procedure set)
begin by mixing the butter and sugar (baking procedure set)
Milk and yogurt are in the dairy section. (grocery store)
dimensions.current: 18-scenario pool, ALL campus/institutional (IT desk, library, planetarium, car rental, theater, gym, health center, chem lab, dining, bookstore, dorm, tutoring, career, gallery, recycling, radio, pool, darkroom). No procedure/how-to sets; no everyday-retail/cooking/repair.
dimensions.gap: gen is a campus-office monoculture; missing the procedure/how-to set family (~12% of real) and everyday-commerce settings

dimensions.name: speaker_role_and_setting_framing

fields: standard instruction 'Listen to the speaker and repeat what (he/she) says. Repeat only once.'
templates: You are working/volunteering/learning ... Your manager is training/teaching you to assist ...

dimensions.detector: field presence (setting); ASR pattern match on framing templates
examples: You are being trained to assist customers at a university tech store.
You are learning how to give the weather report for the University radio station.
You have an internship at a wildlife sanctuary. Your manager is teaching you how to assist visitors there.
dimensions.current: bank stores only `scenario` + `speaker_role`; NO second-person setting sentence and NO 'Listen and repeat... Repeat only once.' instruction
dimensions.gap: generated item cannot be presented with authentic framing; add a second-person `setting` + standard instruction to the record

correlations: 'Difficulty' ≈ word count: real per-sentence difficulty tracks length (easy<=7 / medium 8-12 / hard>=13), not syntax. A long SIMPLE sentence is the real 'hard'. Control length distribution; stop equating hard with conditional/consequence grammar.

The long finish carries the clause break: S7-is-longest (92%) + 75% of long sentences have a comma + closing uses if/when/lastly + map/schedule pointer = ONE authentic move (a single long, comma-split, wayfinding closer). Generating one long fronted-clause closer (and a shorter S6) fixes length_progression, hard_sentence_comma_rate, and closing_wayfinding_trope at once.

Synthetic register cluster = greeting + question + threat + over-'you': s1_opener (over-Welcome), yes_no_questions, direct_address (over-you), punitive_warning_trope co-occur and all trace to the prompt's two worked examples + STRUCTURE_RULES. Fixing the examples and the easy/hard structure lists removes all four together.
deferred: Per-sentence response timing budget (seconds): gen stamps 8/10/12 by tier but real value is unverified. ASR gives only playback gaps between sentences (e.g. 2026-01-21: 4,2,9,6,3,10,6s), which are speak-then-pause intervals and partly batched — NOT the on-screen response window. Do not invent a target; needs video capture of the countdown.

Structural syntax taxonomy (passive/relative/conditional sub-percentages in repeat-flavor-model.json) came from 5 third-party goarno.io sets, not the 51 real sets, and real 'difficulty' is length-based — treat those sub-percentages as unvalidated.
Speaker gender (she x5 / he x3 across 8 labeled sets): suggestive mixed/slightly-female voice pool but n too small and irrelevant to text generation; recorded for completeness.
Exact scenario frequency weights: domain tagging is overlapping/keyword-based and ASR settings known for only 13/51 sets, so the gym>library>store ordering is indicative, not a precise target.
